title: Crunchy Floral Polish Shrimp Salad
description: A nostalgic Polish salad featuring tender sousvade shrimp, crunchy toasted sunflower seeds, and delicate edible flowers, all balanced in a bright, sugarfree vinaigrette.

ingredients.name: Dried shrimp, rehydrated
ingredients.notes: Soaked in warm water 10 min, then drained

ingredients.name: Fresh ground black pepper, to taste

instructions: Rehydrate the dried shrimp by soaking them in warm water for 10 minutes, then drain thoroughly.
Place the fresh shrimp and the rehydrated dried shrimp in a vacuum‑sealed bag with olive oil, a pinch of salt, and pepper; seal the bag.
Cook the bagged shrimp sous‑vade at 54 °C (129 °F) for 15 minutes.
While the shrimp cook, toast the sunflower seeds in a dry skillet over medium heat until golden, about 3–4 minutes; set aside to cool.
Whisk apple cider vinegar, Dijon mustard, salt, and pepper in a small bowl to create the vinaigrette.
When the shrimp are done, remove the bag, pat the shrimp dry, and slice them into bite‑size pieces.
In a serving bowl, combine the shrimp, vinaigrette, chopped parsley, and edible violets; toss gently to coat.
Sprinkle the toasted sunflower seeds on top for added crunch.
Adjust seasoning with extra salt or pepper as desired.
Serve immediately over a bed of mixed greens or cucumber ribbons for extra freshness.
